pages.text: $(li)$(bold)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#basics)–ě—Ā–Ĺ–ĺ–≤—č$()$(li)$(bold)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#thirst)–Ė–į–∂–ī–į$()$(li)$(bold)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#nutrition_screen)–≠–ļ—Ä–į–Ĺ –†–į—Ü–ł–ĺ–Ĺ–į$()$(li)$(bold)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#nutrients)–ü–ł—ā–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ—č–Ķ –≤–Ķ—Č–Ķ—Ā—ā–≤–į$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#fruit)–§—Ä—É–ļ—ā—č$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#vegetables)–ě–≤–ĺ—Č–ł$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#protein)–Ď–Ķ–Ľ–ļ–ł$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#grain)–ó–Ķ—Ä–Ĺ–ĺ–≤—č–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#dairy)–ú–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ—á–Ĺ—č–Ķ$()$(li)$(bold)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#contaminants)–ó–į–≥—Ä—Ź–∑–Ĺ–ł—ā–Ķ–Ľ–ł$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#toxins)–Ę–ĺ–ļ—Ā–ł–Ĺ—č$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#parasites)–ü–į—Ä–į–∑–ł—ā—č$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#microplastics)–ú–ł–ļ—Ä–ĺ–Ņ–Ľ–į—Ā—ā–ł–ļ$()

$(li)$(bold)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#transient_effects)–ü–Ķ—Ä–Ķ—Ö–ĺ–ī—Ź—Č–ł–Ķ —ć—Ą—Ą–Ķ–ļ—ā—č$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#cooling_transient_effect)–ě—Ö–Ľ–į–∂–ī–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#warming_transient_effect)–°–ĺ–≥—Ä–Ķ–≤–į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#quenching_transient_effect)–£—ā–ĺ–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#bolstering_transient_effect)–£–ļ—Ä–Ķ–Ņ–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#hearty_transient_effect)–°—č—ā–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ć$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#rejuvenating_transient_effect)–ě–ľ–ĺ–Ľ–į–∂–ł–≤–į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#freezing_transient_effect)–ó–į–ľ–Ķ—Ä–∑–į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#blazing_transient_effect)–ě–Ī–∂–ł–≥–į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#radiating_transient_effect)–ė–∑–Ľ—É—á–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#nauseating_transient_effect)–ě—ā–≤—Ä–į—Č–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#parching_transient_effect)–°—É—ą–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#deadly_transient_effect)–°–ľ–Ķ—Ä—ā–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ć$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#fulfilling_transient_effect)–£–ī–ĺ–≤–Ľ–Ķ—ā–≤–ĺ—Ä—Ď–Ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ć$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#sugary_transient_effect)–ü—Ä–ł—ā–ĺ—Ä–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ć$()$(li2)$(l:getting_started/food_and_water#spicy_transient_effect)–Ė–≥—É—á–Ķ—Ā—ā—Ć$()

pages.title: –ě—Ā–Ĺ–ĺ–≤—č
pages.text: In TerraFirmaCraft, not only must you manage your hunger, but you must manage your thirst. Hunger works similar to vanilla. Most pieces of food restore about a fifth of your hunger bar. Some pieces of food may restore a bit less, such as $(thing)Cattail Roots$(). Eating food also restores $(thing)Saturation$(), which can be thought of as how full you are. Depleting food or water results in sluggish movement and taking damage. Dying does not reset your nutrients or contaminants.

–Ě–Ķ–ļ–ĺ—ā–ĺ—Ä—č–Ķ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–ī—É–ļ—ā—č –Ņ–ł—ā–į–Ĺ–ł—Ź –ĺ—á–Ķ–Ĺ—Ć –Ņ–ł—ā–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ—č, –Ņ–ĺ—ć—ā–ĺ–ľ—É –ĺ–Ĺ–ł –ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ć—ą–Ķ –Ĺ–Ķ –ī–į–ī—É—ā –≤–į–ľ –≥–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–ī–į—ā—Ć, –≤ —ā–ĺ –≤—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ź –ļ–į–ļ –Ĺ–Ķ–ļ–ĺ—ā–ĺ—Ä—č–Ķ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–ī—É–ļ—ā—č –ł–ľ–Ķ—é—ā –Ľ–ł—ą—Ć –ļ—Ä–į—ā–ļ–ĺ–≤—Ä–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–Ķ –≤–ĺ–∑–ī–Ķ–Ļ—Ā—ā–≤–ł–Ķ.$(br2)–®–ļ–į–Ľ–į –≥–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ–ī–į —É–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ć—ą–į–Ķ—ā—Ā—Ź –≤–ĺ –≤—Ä–Ķ–ľ—Ź –ĺ–Ī—č—á–Ĺ–ĺ–Ļ –ł–≥—Ä—č. –ě–Ĺ–į —É–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ć—ą–į–Ķ—ā—Ā—Ź –Ī—č—Ā—ā—Ä–Ķ–Ķ, –Ķ—Ā–Ľ–ł –≤—č —Ā–Ņ—Ä–ł–Ĺ—ā—É–Ķ—ā–Ķ, –Ņ–Ľ–į–≤–į–Ķ—ā–Ķ –ł–Ľ–ł $(l:getting_started/size_and_weight#overburdening)–ü–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–≥—Ä—É–∂–Ķ–Ĺ—č$(). –ė–Ĺ—Ą–ĺ—Ä–ľ–į—Ü–ł—é –ĺ $(thing)–Ě–į—Ā—č—Č–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–ł$(), $(thing)–í–ĺ–ī–Ķ$(), –ł –į —ā–į–ļ–∂–Ķ $(thing)–Ě—É—ā—Ä–ł–Ķ–Ĺ—ā–į—Ö$(), –ī–ĺ—Ā—ā—É–Ņ–Ĺ—č—Ö –ł–∑ –Ķ–ī—č, –ľ–ĺ–∂–Ĺ–ĺ –Ņ–ĺ–Ľ—É—á–ł—ā—Ć, –Ĺ–į–≤–Ķ–ī—Ź –ļ—É—Ä—Ā–ĺ—Ä –Ĺ–į –Ĺ–Ķ–Ķ –≤ –ł–Ĺ–≤–Ķ–Ĺ—ā–į—Ä–Ķ. –° –Ņ–ĺ–ľ–ĺ—Č—Ć—é $(item)$(k:key.sneak)$() –ĺ—ā–ļ—Ä—č–≤–į–Ķ—ā—Ā—Ź –Ņ–ĺ–Ľ–Ĺ—č–Ļ –Ĺ–į–Ī–ĺ—Ä –Ņ–ĺ–ī—Ā–ļ–į–∑–ĺ–ļ.

All foods have a tooltip with this information. The tooltip includes the $(l:mechanics/decay)Decay Date$() of the food, which may be extended with preservation. When viewing this information, it's important to realize that not all foods have nutritional value. For example, $(l:mechanics/bread)Dough$() is a food, but it has no Nutrition, Saturation, or Water value. Eating it would not do much good.

A food tooltip may look like:$(br2)$(bold)Orange$()$(br)  $(2)$(bold)Expires on: 11:59 July 6, 1004 (in 1 month(s) and 1 day(s))$()$(br)  $(8)Nutrition:$(br)  - Saturation: 2%$(br)  - Water: 10%$(br)$(a)  - Fruit: 0.5

Thirst is the level of water in your body. It depletes at a similar rate to hunger. At high temperatures or levels of high hunger usage, your thirst will deplete faster. Luckily, drinking $(thing)Fresh Water$() replenishes thirst. This can be done by clicking $(item)$(k:key.use)$() on a water block. Drinking saltwater causes you to lose thirst, and even has a chance of giving you the $(thing)Thirst$() effect, which will drain you even more.
pages.title: –Ė–į–∂–ī–į

pages.name: –Ď–Ķ–∑–ĺ–Ņ–į—Ā–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ć –≤–ĺ–ī—č
pages.text: To avoid saltwater or muddy water, look for rivers, lakes, and freshwater plants like $(thing)Cattails$().

$(l:getting_started/pottery)Knapping$() and firing a $(l:getting_started/pottery#jug)jug$() is a way to carry water with you. Fill it like a bucket with $(item)$(k:key.use)$(). Holding $(item)$(k:key.use)$() drinks the water.
You can also drink out of other containers such as $(l:waterflasks)Flasks$() or $(l:mechanics/glassworking)Glass Bottles$().

In $(bold)$(#ff0fb7)TerraFirma$()$()$(bold)$(#595959)Greg$()$() the nutrition screen displays vast information about your health. $(li)There is a main graph displaying your $(l:getting_started/food_and_water#nutrients)Nutrients$()$()$(li)A side graph showing your $(l:getting_started/food_and_water#contaminants)Contaminants$()$()$(li)A table displaying your health stats.$()$(li)And a toggleable view for checking on your teams nutrition.$()$(br)$(item)Hold-$(k:key.sneak)$() over an element on the screen to view more information.$()
pages.title: –≠–ļ—Ä–į–Ĺ –†–į—Ü–ł–ĺ–Ĺ–į

pages.text: The nutrition screen, with graphs showing the levels of each nutrient.

There are five nutrients, all obtainable from food: $(l)$(a)Fruit$()$(), $(l)$(2)Vegetables$()$(), $(l)$(c)Protein$()$(), $(l)$(6)Grain$()$(), and $(l)$(5)Dairy$()$(). Eating food gives you its nutrients. Meals such as $(l:mechanics/pot#soup)Soup$() combine more nutrients into one meal. Often meals will borrow the nutrients from their ingredients directly. This is important, because meals you ate a while ago don't count towards your nutrition.
pages.title: –ü–ł—ā–į—ā–Ķ–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ—č–Ķ –≤–Ķ—Č–Ķ—Ā—ā–≤–į

pages.text: The total value of your nutrients determines your maximum HP. At max value you can achieve $(l)$(c)30 hearts$()$(). While the lowest is $(l)$(c)3 hearts$()$().$(br)Your average nutrients also effect the following:$()$(li)$(bold)Average >55% ‚Üī$()$()$(italic)$(li2)Increase passive health regen by 50%.$()$()$(italic)$(li2)Decreases $(l:tfg_ores/hazards#medicine)Medical Condition$() progression rate by 25%.$()$()$(italic)$(li2)Increase medical condition passive healing rate by 50%.$()$()

$(li)$(bold)Average >85% ‚Üī$()$()$(italic)$(li2)Increase passive health regen by 100%.$()$()$(italic)$(li2)Decreases $(l:tfg_ores/hazards#medicine)Medical Condition$() progression rate by 50%.$()$()$(italic)$(li2)Increase medical condition passive healing rate by 100%.$()$()

pages.title: –§—Ä—É–ļ—ā—č
pages.text: $(l)$(a)Fruit$()$() nutrients are mostly found from $(l:the_world/wild_fruits)Fruiting Plants$(), like berry bushes, and fruit trees. A notable exception to this is $(l:mechanics/crops#pumpkin)Pumpkins$() and $(l:mechanics/crops#pumpkin)Melons$(), which nutritionally are fruits.$(br2)Increasing your fruit value gives the following effects:$()$(li)$(bold)Average >55% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Decreases thirst usage by 25%.$()$(li)$(bold)Average >85% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Increases mining speed by 30%.$()

pages.title: –ě–≤–ĺ—Č–ł
pages.text: $(l)$(2)Vegetables$()$() are found in nearly every $(l:mechanics/crops)Crop$().$(br2)Increasing your vegetable value gives the following effects:$()$(li)$(bold)Value >55% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Removes under-water mining penalty.$()$(li)$(bold)Value >85% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Gives the respiration effect.$()

pages.title: –Ď–Ķ–Ľ–ļ–ł
pages.text: $(l)$(c)Protein$()$() can be sourced from the meat of $(l:the_world/wild_animals)Animals$(). It can also be obtained from some crops like $(thing)legumes$().$(br2)Increasing your protein value gives the following effects:$()$(li)$(bold)Value >55% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Decrease exhaustion multiplier by 25%.$()$(li)$(bold)Value >85% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Allows you to carry an additional huge & heavy item without being $(l:getting_started/size_and_weight#overburdening)Overburdened$() (like a sealed barrel).$()

pages.title: –ó–Ķ—Ä–Ĺ–ĺ–≤—č–Ķ
pages.text: $(l)$(6)Grain$()$() is found in cereal crops, such as $(l:mechanics/crops#barley)Barley$(). The processing of grain is on the $(l:firmalife/bread)Bread$() page. Some root crops also count as grains.$(br2)Increasing your grain value gives the following effects:$()$(li)$(bold)Value >55% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Gives a 5% speed buff.$()$(li)$(bold)Value >85% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Gives a 15% speed buff.$()

pages.title: –ú–ĺ–Ľ–ĺ—á–Ĺ—č–Ķ
pages.text: $(l)$(5)Dairy$()$() comes from $(thing)Milk$(), which is derived from $(l:mechanics/animal_husbandry#dairy_animals)Dairy Animals$(). Processing and drinking milk is covered on the $(l:firmalife/cheese)Dairy$() page.$(br2)Increasing your dairy value gives the following effects:$()$(li)$(bold)Value >55% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Gives the $(thing)Feather Falling I$() effect.$()$(li)$(bold)Value >85% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Gives the $(thing)Feather Falling IV$() effect.$()

In addition to nutrients $(bold)$(#ff0fb7)TerraFirma$()$()$(bold)$(#595959)Greg$()$() also adds contaminants. There are three contaminants, all obtainable from food: $(l)$(d)Toxins$()$(), $(l)$(4)Parasites$()$(), and $(l)$(3)Microplastics$()$(). Contaminants can be passed into meal items and build up within the player.
pages.title: –ó–į–≥—Ä—Ź–∑–Ĺ–ł—ā–Ķ–Ľ–ł
–Ę–ĺ–ļ—Ā–ł–Ĺ—č
pages.text: $(l)$(d)Toxins$()$() come from specifically dangerous crops and meats. Toxins decrease naturally at a rate of 3%/day. Consume $(item)Detox Capsules$() to actively $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#cure_toxins)Cure Toxins$().$(br2)Increasing your toxins value gives the following effects:$()$(li)$(bold)Value >0% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)On any new increase: 25% chance to get a random hazardous effect for 10sec.$()$(li)$(bold)Value >99% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)$(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#final_moments)Death.$()

pages.title: –ü–į—Ä–į–∑–ł—ā—č
pages.text: $(l)$(4)Parasites$()$() come from eating certain raw meats or drinking muddy water. Consume $(item)Parasiticide Capsules$() to actively $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#cure_parasites)Cure Parasites$().$(br2)Increasing your parasites value gives the following effects:$()$(li)$(bold)Value >10% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)20% chance to increase by 10% each day to a max of 60%.$()$(li)$(bold)Value >33% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Increases passive exhaustion by 25%.$()$(li)$(bold)Value >66% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Increases passive exhaustion by 50%.$()

pages.title: –ú–ł–ļ—Ä–ĺ–Ņ–Ľ–į—Ā—ā–ł–ļ
pages.text: $(l)$(3)Microplastics$()$() come from consuming food packaged in plastic. Microplastics decrease naturally at a rate of 1%/day. Consume $(item)Nanofiltration Capsules$() to actively $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#cure_microplastics)Cure Microplastics$().$(br)Increasing your microplastics value gives the following effects:$()$(li)$(bold)Value >33% ‚Üí $()$()$(italic)Increase high-temp thirst decay multiplier by 25%$()$(li)$(bold)Value >66% ‚Üī$()$()$(italic)$(li2)Increase high-temp thirst decay multiplier by 50%.$()$()$(italic)$(li2)On any new increase: 5% chance to get $(l:tfg_ores/hazards#Carcinogens)Cancer$().$()$()

$(bold)$(#ff0fb7)TerraFirma$()$()$(bold)$(#595959)Greg$()$() also adds $(item)Transient Effects$(). Transient effects exist for the purpose of transferring buffs/ debuffs from ingredients into meal items. Transient effects can be combined and consumed. However, they do not accumulate within the player‚ÄēThey are immediately consumed.$(br)The strength of the effect depends on the value of the nutrient. However, the way the strength is determined is not uniform for every type.$()
pages.title: –ü–Ķ—Ä–Ķ—Ö–ĺ–ī—Ź—Č–ł–Ķ —ć—Ą—Ą–Ķ–ļ—ā—č

pages.text: $(#12d7ff)‚ĚÖ$()$(bold) Cooling$()$(li)Gives the player the $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#cooling)Cooling$() effect with 1 minute of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 30min.$()

$(#ff7700)ūüĒÜ$()$(bold) Warming$()$(li)Gives the player the $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#warming)Warming$() effect with 1 minute of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 30min.$()

$(#00c792)ūüćĻ$()$(bold) Quenching$()$(li)Gives the player the $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#quenched)Quenched$() effect with 1 minute of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 30min.$()
$(#ffe417)‚ô°$()$(bold) Bolstering$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Absorption$() effect for 8 minutes. With its amplifier scaled based on the amount of nutrient points with a max amplifier of 5.$()
$(#ff171b)‚ô°$()$(bold) Hearty$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Health Boost$() effect for 8 minutes. With its amplifier scaled based on the amount of nutrient points with a max amplifier of 5.$()
$(#ff178f)ūüíē$()$(bold) Rejuvenating$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Regeneration$() effect with 1 minute of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 3min.$()

$(#12d7ff)‚õá$()$(bold) Freezing$()$(li)Gives the player the $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#freezing)Freezing$() effect with 5 seconds of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 30sec.$()

$(#ff7700)ūüĒ•$()$(bold) Blazing$()$(li)Gives the player the $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#blazing)Blazing$() effect with 5 seconds of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 30sec.$()

$(#9fc700)‚ėĘ$()$(bold) Radiating$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Glowing$() effect for 24 seconds.$()$(li)Gives the player the $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#instant_radiation)Radiation$() effect for 24 seconds. With its amplifier scaled based on the amount of nutrient points with a max amplifier of 5.$()

$(#078f00)ūü§Ę$()$(bold) Nauseating$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Nausea$() effect with 5 seconds of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 12sec.$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Hunger VI$() effect with 10 seconds of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 30sec.$()
ūüßā$(bold) Parching$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Thirsty$() effect with 1 minute of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 8min.$()

$(#470275)ūüíÄ$()$(bold) Deadly$()$(li)Gives the player the $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#final_moments)Final Moments$() effect for 8min. With every additional nutrient point halving the duration.$()

$(#551913)ūüćĖ$()$(bold) Fulfilling$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Resistance$() effect with 1 minute of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 15min.$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Instant Health$() effect. With its amplifier scaled based on the amount of nutrient points with a max amplifier of 2.$()

$(#837447)ūüćį$()$(bold) Sugary$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Weakness$() effect with 15 seconds of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 5min.$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Speed$() effect with 1 minute of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 30min.$()

$(#8F0000)ūüĆ∂ $()$(bold) Spicy$()$(li)Gives the player the $(n)Thirsty$() effect with 15 seconds of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 5min.$()$(li)Gives the player the $(l:tfg_tips/mob_effects_glossary#warming)Warming$() mob effect with 1 minute of duration per 1 nutrient point. With a max duration of 30min.$()
